title: coreopsis delphiniifolia
intro: Coreopsis delphiniifolia, also known as the sea bluff coreopsis, is a perennial wildflower native to the Pacific coast of North America, characterized by its delicate yellow petals and fern-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Coreopsis delphiniifolia include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in some traditional medicine practices, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
